Post_Text: A rebate is supposed to be a discount for your plan. In a traditional PBM contract, the bigger the drug's price, the bigger the rebate the middleman keeps. So the system has a reason to favor the expensive drug. Here is where your discount actually went: [LINK]

An admin fee advertised at four dollars per member can land north of ten once every surcharge is counted. Clawbacks. DIR fees. Charges that grow after the sale. None of it shows up where you would look. The fees you cannot find on the invoice: [LINK]

There is a PBM model where the fee is flat and disclosed, the spread is gone, and 100% of rebates flow back to your plan. It chases one number: lowest net cost. Schools your size run it today. Yours probably does not, and here is why: [LINK]
